business_angle: Lawrence (the Bristol sparky) is the high-end domestic electrician for Bristol homes who wants the work done to a refined, considered standard - lighting and downlights planned and fitted, consumer units brought up to scratch first, faults found and explained - by someone who stays across the latest gear rather than fitting the same old stuff.
why_this_trader_wins: The reviews describe a premium, design-minded domestic specialist, not a call-out generalist. Bel: 'his work is impeccable ... he stays across industry innovation and development so he is always able to offer the latest solutions instead of using the same old stuff'. Emily: 'did a fantastic job fitting some lights for us ... done to a really high quality'. Marc: he spotted an outdated consumer unit and explained it needed replacing before the rest of the lighting and socket work could be done. Matthieu and Megan: clear quoting, takes immense pride, 'impeccably tidy'. Five-star across 135 Google reviews is the strongest proof in the batch.
target_customer: Bristol homeowners (BS16 Downend / Bromley Heath and wider Bristol) doing a considered domestic job - lighting and downlights, a rewire or new circuits, a consumer-unit upgrade, or a fault chased down - who want it planned, explained, and finished to a high standard rather than rushed.
page_single_job: Get that homeowner to request a quote (or call Lawrence) for a domestic electrical job - lighting, rewire, consumer unit, or fault-finding.

customer_praise_themes.theme: lighting and downlights fitted to a high standard
customer_praise_themes.evidence: brief.json reviews[2] (Emily): 'did a fantastic job fitting some lights for us ... The work he did was exactly as we asked and done to a really high quality'; reviews[0] (Marc): 'bathroom downlights, and a garden light and outdoor socket'

named_people.name: Lawrence
named_people.basis: brief.json contact_name 'Lawrence', contact_name_usage_allowed=true, contact_name_confidence=high, named in 5 of 5 Google reviews; the personal brand is 'Bristolsparky' (the Linktree handle, brief.json website_url). Use Lawrence's first name freely in site prose. This is the Kyle Knowles ARCH-2 / contact_name_usage_allowed precedent applied directly (here the flag is already true).

strongest_review_quote.text: Lawrence is consistently brilliant. Not only is his work impeccable but he is a pleasure to have around. He also stays across industry innovation and development so he is always able to offer the latest solutions instead of using the same old stuff.
strongest_review_quote.author: Bel

real_services.name: Lighting and downlights
real_services.evidence: reviews[2] Emily (fitting lights, high quality); reviews[0] Marc (bathroom downlights, garden light, hallway light switch); photos[] outdoor switched spur + sockets. This is the lead service per the high-end-domestic-lighting angle.

real_services.name: Consumer units and fuse boards
real_services.evidence: reviews[0] Marc (outdated consumer unit replaced before other work); photos[] 01-places.webp completed consumer unit board

real_services.name: Rewires and new circuits
real_services.evidence: reviews[0] Marc (outdoor socket, hallway switch, garden light - new points and circuits); reviews[1] Bel ('all domestic and commercial jobs', unusual/complicated job); photos[] sockets being installed, earthing/bonding

real_services.name: Fault-finding and repairs
real_services.evidence: reviews[4] Megan (sorting out dodgy electrics in study); photos[] 04-places.webp multifunction tester in use

claims_to_avoid: any certification or membership badge (NICEIC, NAPIT, Part P, Gas Safe, Checkatrade Vetted, TrustATrader, Which? Trusted Trader) - none verified; brief.json directory_probes show 0 verified profiles (Yell/MyBuilder/Trustpilot snippet-only single-signal; TrustATrader was a rejected homonym; Checkatrade/RatedPeople/Bark/Houzz/MyJobQuote NOT_FOUND). Banned by copy_voice_constants CLAIMED_BADGE_PATTERNS unless evidenced.

'fully insured' / 'qualified' / 'certified electrician' / 'registered' claims - not evidenced (no certification in brief.certifications, none verified). Note: 'tested', 'testing' as a described activity is fine (photo shows a multifunction tester) but never a certification claim.

the Google-inferred service 'Bathroom installations' as a service - it is brief.json services[0] but inferred=true, source=review, direct=false; Lawrence is an electrician, the bathroom mention traces to bathroom downlights, not bathroom fitting. Do NOT list bathroom installation as a service.
the street address '4 Bromley Heath Ave' anywhere public - use Bristol / BS16 (Downend, Bromley Heath) only

service areas beyond Bristol and the plausible BS postcodes around BS16 - the brief service_area chips (Redfield, St George, Easton, Bedminster, Clifton, Kingswood, Fishponds) are Google-Maps chips, not coverage; treat as data, replace with a derived BS-district list around the BS16 base (no national footprint for a one-van trade)

any commercial-led positioning - Bel mentions 'domestic and commercial', so commercial work may be acknowledged once, but the angle and target customer are high-end DOMESTIC; do not lead on commercial

claiming the Linktree / any existing website as proof - it is a link aggregator (linktr.ee/Bristolsparky), classified HAS_REAL_SITE in enrich but it is NOT a real site; this build replaces it. Do not reference it.
personality: refined, calm, precise; an electrician who plans the job, sorts the safety first, fits good kit to a high standard, and leaves it tidy. Premium without being showy. Lawrence talks straight and explains his recommendations.
evidence_strength: rich-content / single-source (5 detailed five-star Google reviews + 9 completed-project photos; no verified third-party profile - directory probes unverified, Facebook/Instagram not found). Strong, specific, usable; same posture as the Kyle Knowles and Damo reference builds (source_quality FAIL/PASS_WITH_WARNINGS).
pitch_hook_summary: Lead on the high-end-domestic angle: lighting and downlights planned and fitted to a high standard, consumer units and safety sorted first, latest solutions rather than the same old stuff, finished impeccably tidy - backed by a clean 5.0 from 135 Google reviews.

metadata.source_quality_reason: HAS_REAL_SITE_SKIP - the detected 'real site' is a Linktree link-aggregator (linktr.ee/Bristolsparky), not a website. Per the assignment, the Linktree is not a real site; noted and continued. Lead validity HAS_REAL_SITE_SKIP, contactability=NEEDS_MANUAL_REVIEW (no email, UK mobile only, manual WhatsApp). This is a BUILD run; pitch_gate enforces at outreach time, not build time.
metadata.manual_review_note: Same usable-evidence posture as the Kyle Knowles reference (also source_quality=FAIL). Evidence is specific and rich. Deploy will require --allow-manual-review to clear the NEEDS_MANUAL_REVIEW state.
metadata.authored_blind: Path B authored from the frozen brief + CLAUDE.md + the webfortrades-site-design skill. Pre-allocated design seed honoured: jewel-tone family (deep amethyst violet, NOT green, NOT AC's aubergine+brass eggplant; cool chrome/pewter metal not brass), body=IBM Plex Sans, display=serif-editorial (Playfair Display). Distinct from all 9 prior deployed sites - see design-direction.md distinctness check.
